compiled_state.title: record-test-status.sh uses stale cached results after .test-index RED marker removal
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

After removing a stale RED marker from .test-index, record-test-status.sh continued to report the marker as stale because it was resuming from cached test session state. The cached state included the old diff hash and test results from before the .test-index edit.

## Root cause

record-test-status.sh uses a test-session-state.json file for resume-on-timeout. When .test-index is edited, the cached state becomes invalid but the script does not detect this. It resumes from the cached results which still contain the stale marker detection.

## Workaround

Delete both test-gate-status and test-session-state.json from the artifacts directory before re-running record-test-status.sh. Note: deleting test-gate-status triggers the bypass sentinel hook, so this must be done carefully.

## Fix needed

record-test-status.sh should invalidate its cached state when .test-index has been modified since the cache was written (compare .test-index mtime or content hash against the cached state).

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC) — severity:1 (functional degradation), complexity:1 (single file, multiple touch points), environment:0 (local), intermittent:0 (deterministic)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
